name: cuML (NVIDIA RAPIDS)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); requires NVIDIA GPU; no API costs

auth.notes: Local GPU library; no auth required

performance.notes: GPU-accelerated ML algorithms 10-100x faster than scikit-learn for large datasets

agent_readiness.notes: NVIDIA RAPIDS GPU-accelerated machine learning library with scikit-learn-compatible API. Implements common ML algorithms on GPU: KMeans, DBSCAN, RandomForest, PCA, UMAP, t-SNE, logistic regression, SVM, etc. Drop-in scikit-learn replacement for supported algorithms. Best combined with cuDF for full GPU data science pipeline.

gotchas: NVIDIA GPU required; algorithm support less complete than scikit-learn
Not all scikit-learn algorithms implemented — gaps require CPU fallback
CUDA version dependencies need careful management
Estimator API deviates from scikit-learn in some edge cases
best_when: Large-scale tabular ML where scikit-learn is a bottleneck (millions of samples)
Dimensionality reduction (UMAP, t-SNE) on very large datasets
KMeans/DBSCAN clustering on GPU for fast exploratory analysis
Full GPU ML pipeline: cuDF preprocessing → cuML training
avoid_when: Algorithms not yet implemented in cuML (long list of gaps)
summary: cuML is NVIDIA RAPIDS' GPU-accelerated ML library with a scikit-learn-compatible API. Implements common algorithms (KMeans, RandomForest, PCA, UMAP, t-SNE, logistic regression) on NVIDIA GPUs, providing 10-100x speedup for large datasets. Drop-in scikit-learn replacement for supported algorithms. Best combined with cuDF for end-to-end GPU data science. NVIDIA GPU required.
